prompt: Photorealistic portrait of a man in his late 30s seated in the open cockpit of an F-15E Strike Eagle on the tarmac. His body and torso are oriented naturally forward toward the instrument panel and HUD, sitting upright in the ejection seat with both legs positioned correctly in the cockpit footwells, knees pointing forward. He has turned his head to his left to look directly at the camera, creating a natural over-the-shoulder head turn. Full USAF olive drab flight suit with squadron patches and G-suit leg panels covering both legs properly, oxygen mask unclipped and hanging to the side. He is wearing a realistic modern USAF HGU-55/P helmet fitted with the Joint Helmet Mounted Cueing System (JHMCS): the helmet has a hard matte grey shell with visible electromagnetic head-tracking sensor emitter pods mounted on the exterior of the helmet, a JHMCS monocular display unit mounted on the right visor rail (a small rectangular projection box that sits above the right eye), a dark tinted visor raised fully up showing his face, and the overall helmet is visually complex and angular with mounted hardware — not a plain smooth helmet. His face must exactly match the reference photo: short buzzed dark hair with subtle stubble on the scalp, very short dark stubble beard, brown eyes, olive Mediterranean skin tone, warm relaxed smile, and importantly a nose that is compact and proportionate — not elongated at the tip. Shot with 85mm lens at f/2.0, ISO 200, bright afternoon sunlight from camera-right. Cockpit background shows authentic F-15E instrument panels, MFDs, HUD, throttle quadrant, tarmac and airfield visible beyond the cockpit. Realistic skin texture — visible pores, no smoothing. Do not alter his facial features. Do not change his hair or beard. Do not elongate the nose. Identity must match reference photograph exactly.
negative_prompt: plain smooth helmet, simple helmet, no helmet hardware, wrong helmet, elongated nose, long nose tip, pointy nose, different person, wrong face, bald head, full beard, body facing camera, torso twisted toward camera, wrong leg position, smooth skin, plastic skin, airbrushed, beauty filter, CGI, 3D render, cartoon, visor down, closed visor, studio backdrop, body proportion averaging, anatomy normalization, symmetrical perfection, skin smoothing, watermark, text overlay
